originalText: The briefing asserts that the new infrastructure plan will create 1 million jobs within the next year, cut average commute times by 40%, and be fully funded without increasing taxes or public debt.

claims.claimText: create 1 million jobs within the next year
fully funded without increasing taxes or public debt

sources.name: Ministry of Labor Forecast
sources.evidence: Projects job creation but with wide uncertainty intervals.

sources.name: Transport Modeling Report
sources.evidence: Estimated commute reduction between 8–15% in major cities.

verificationResults.summary: Impact on commute times is overstated relative to official modeling.

sources.name: Budget Office Analysis
sources.evidence: Financing mix includes new bond issuance and reallocated taxes.

sources.name: Policy Fact-Check
sources.evidence: Experts disagree on long-term debt impact.
